item.title: Iranian Threat Actor MERCURY Exploits Log4j 2 Vulnerabilities
item.description: The Iranian threat actor MERCURY has been exploiting unpatched Log4j 2 vulnerabilities in SysAid applications to target organizations.

item.title: CISA Warning of Palo Alto Networks' PAN-OS Vulnerability
item.description: This blog is about CISA's warning regarding the active exploitation of a Palo Alto Networks PAN-OS vulnerability.

item.title: WordPress Sites Hacked with Fake Cloudflare DDoS Alerts - ThreatMon
item.description: WordPress Sites Hacked with Fake Cloudflare DDoS Alerts: Attackers are tricking users into downloading malware.

item.title: 13 Organizations Targeted by Chinese-Linked APT41
item.description: APT41, a Chinese state-sponsored hacking group, has targeted 13 organizations, deploying Cobalt Strike for command and control.

item.title: Google's Update for the New Chrome Zero-Day Vulnerability
item.description: Google has released an emergency update for Chrome to address a high-severity zero-day vulnerability actively exploited in the wild.

item.title: Kaspersky Published a Report on Malicious Browser Extensions
item.description: Kaspersky's report highlights the rise of malicious browser extensions that steal cryptocurrency and perform web injects.

item.title: Microsoft Has Disrupted SEABORGIUM Phishing Operation
item.description: Microsoft has disrupted SEABORGIUM, a Russian-linked threat actor known targeting NATO countries involved in Ukraine conflict.

item.title: PyPI Package 'secretslib' Drops Fileless Cryptominer to Linux
item.description: PyPI Package ‘secretslib’ was discovered to covertly execute a Monero cryptominer directly in memory on Linux systems.

item.title: USA Offers $10M Bounty for The Conti Ransomware Gang
item.description: USA has announced a reward of up to $10 million for information about key members of the Conti ransomware group.

item.title: Cisco Has Been Hacked by Yanluowang Ransomware Group - ThreatMon
item.description: In May 2022, Cisco's corporate network was breached by the Yanluowang ransomware group, who accessed an employee's VPN account.

item.title: SQL Injection Failure and Flaws Have Been Patched by Vmware
item.description: SQL Injection Failure and Flaws That Allow Remote Attackers to Execute Code Remotely Have Been Patched by Vmware

item.title: What is the OpenSSL Version Vulnerability? - ThreatMon Blog
item.description: Learn about the OpenSSL version vulnerability, a flaw in the popular encryption library that can expose sensitive data.

item.title: All You Need to Know About Cyber Threat Intelligence
item.description: Understand cyber threat intelligence, a vital cybersecurity process involving the collection and analysis of data on potential cyber threats.

item.title: Top 10 Dark Web Search Engines - ThreatMon Blog
item.description: Discover the 10 best Dark Web search engines for safe, anonymous browsing of hidden sites and resources...

item.title: APT39: Cyber Espionage Network in Iran's Shadow - ThreatMon Blog
item.description: APT39, also known as Chafer or Remix Kitten, is an Iranian cyber espionage group active since at least 2014.

item.title: Top 10 Dark Web Forums - ThreatMon Blog
item.description: Explore the top 10 dark web forums, their roles in cybercrime, the types of illicit activities and their impact on global cybersecurity.

item.title: Lotus Panda: China's Cyber Power - ThreatMon Blog
item.description: Examine Lotus Panda, an alleged Chinese state-sponsored cyber espionage group, known for targeting global infrastructures and data theft.

item.title: Iran-Based APTs - ThreatMon Blog
item.description: Delve into Iran-based APTs, exploring their cyber strategies, targeted sectors and the implications for global cybersecurity.

item.title: What is External Attack Surface Management (EASM)?
item.description: Examine External Attack Surface Management (EASM), a cybersecurity discipline to mitigate potential cyber threats.

item.title: Axiom APT Group - ThreatMon Blog
item.description: Investigate the Axiom APT group, a team of cyber attackers known for their strategic targeting of governmental and private sector networks.

item.title: APT44: The Famous Sandworm of Russia - ThreatMon Blog
item.description: Explore APT44, the notorious Russian cyber espionage group known as Sandworm, detailing their methods and targets.

item.title: Top 10 Signs Your Data May be on the Dark Web - ThreatMon Blog
item.description: Your Data May be on the Dark Web: In this extensive guide, let’s discuss ten signs that show your personal data has been stolen.

item.title: Peek into Monthly Vulnerabilities: May 2024 - ThreatMon Blog
item.description: Review the significant security vulnerabilities May 2024, focusing on their potential impacts and critical updates needed to mitigate risks.

item.title: Hunter's Lens: Ransomware 2.0: Beyond Encryption to Extortion
item.description: Delve into the evolution of ransomware 2.0 phase, where tactics extend beyond data encryption to include extortion.

item.title: Top 10 Tools for Effective Attack Surface Management
item.description: Top 10 Tools for Effective Attack Surface Management: Highlighting their features and benefits for enhanced cybersecurity.

item.title: Polyfill.io Supply Chain Attack | ThreatMon Blog
item.description: Learn how the Polyfill.io supply chain attack impacted over 100,000 websites and what steps you can take to secure your site.

item.description: Explore the role of stealer logs in the threat landscape, how they compromise data security and the strategies to mitigate their impact.

item.description: Vulnerabilities June 2024: Examine the key vulnerabilities and security threats identified, underlining their potential impacts.

item.description: Learn about the top ten phishing techniques prevalent in 2024, emphasizing new tactics and preventive strategies to guard against them.

item.title: CrowdStrike Update Halts the Systems: CyberSpace Ripple Effect
item.description: Discuss the unintended consequences of a CrowdStrike update that temporarily halted systems, analyzing its ripple effects across cyberspace.

item.title: Kematian Stealer Technical Analysis - ThreatMon
item.description: Blog In the ever-evolving world of cybersecurity, new threats emerge constantly, challenging our defenses and requiring continuous vigilance. One such threat that has recently come to light is the “Kematian Stealer,” an advanced information-stealing malware. ThreatMon’s Kematian Stealer Technical Analysis Report aims to provide an in-depth analysis of this potent malware, its features, and the […]

item.title: How GitHub Monitoring Enhances Security Postures - ThreatMon
item.description: Explore the transition from code monitoring to threat intelligence on GitHub, demonstrating how it bolsters organizational security postures.

item.title: Proactive Threat Detection for a Leading Manufacturer: How ThreatMon Prevented a Potential Breach - ThreatMon
item.description: Case Study Executive Summary A manufacturing client, previously using a competitor’s threat intelligence solution, discovered the value of ThreatMon when, during a Proof of Concept (POC), we identified a critical backdoor vulnerability on their website. This early detection allowed the company to prevent a security breach from a known entry point that had been exploited […]

item.title: Proactive Dark Web Monitoring: How ThreatMon Prevented a Potential Ransomware Attack in the Energy Sector - ThreatMon
item.description: Case Study Executive Summary ThreatMon’s advanced Dark Web Monitoring capabilities recently helped an energy sector client prevent a potentially devastating security breach. During routine dark web surveillance, ThreatMon identified a stealer log for sale on a black market containing admin credentials to the client’s internal systems. Fortunately, the seller appeared unaware of the log’s true […]

item.title: Strengthening Retail Security: How ThreatMon Protected a Client’s Critical Assets with Attack Surface Management - ThreatMon
item.description: Case Study Executive Summary In a recent success, ThreatMon’s Attack Surface Management module helped a retail client secure a critical vulnerability by identifying an open port that exposed an office security camera to the internet. This proactive detection greatly increased the client’s confidence in ThreatMon, as they recognized the potential implications of such an exposure. […]

item.title: Securing Telecommunications: How ThreatMon’s Fraud Protection Module Prevented Financial Loss and Brand Damage - ThreatMon
item.description: Case Study Executive Summary ThreatMon’s Fraud Protection capabilities recently played a pivotal role in helping a telecommunications client avert significant financial loss and protect their brand reputation. During routine monitoring, ThreatMon detected fraudulent activity involving account takeovers and unauthorized access attempts aimed at exploiting customer accounts for financial gain. By quickly identifying these suspicious patterns, […]

item.title: Securing a Healthcare Giant: How ThreatMon’s Risk Scoring Protects Patients and Partners Across a Vast Network of Suppliers - ThreatMon
item.description: Case Study Executive Summary A prominent healthcare provider, with a sprawling network of suppliers, faced the complex challenge of ensuring cybersecurity across every link in its supply chain. ThreatMon’s risk scoring solution provided the visibility, insight, and rapid response needed to safeguard sensitive patient data and maintain operational continuity. By leveraging ThreatMon’s dynamic risk scoring […]

item.title: From Alarm Overload to Targeted Response: How ThreatMon Enhanced Security for a Government Agency with Millions of Assets - ThreatMon
item.description: Case Study Executive Summary A government organization with millions of digital assets sought an advanced cybersecurity solution to overcome alarm fatigue, which was overwhelming its team using a competitor’s platform. During the Proof of Concept (POC) with ThreatMon, our AI-driven alarm risk scoring feature proved essential, enabling the agency to prioritize alerts based on risk […]
